extraction.one_line_summary: BOASSON HAGEN Edvald attacked alone at 2km out of a breakaway, exploiting a right-side roundabout line that required no braking to gap faster sprinters.
tactical_patterns: Boasson Hagen used roundabout geometry 3km out taking right line requiring zero braking while chasers decelerated left, gaining decisive 50m gap.
Breakaway companions attacked Boasson Hagen repeatedly before finale knowing he was fastest sprinter, but he closed every gap solo without teammates.
Sky positioned Froome safely 4min behind breakaway stage despite 23sec GC lead, prioritizing crash avoidance over stage control before decisive ITT.

rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: positive: Showed surplus power closing repeated attacks solo then accelerating again at 2km, demonstrating sprint+endurance hybrid rare in breakaways
rider_signals.ARNDT Nicholas: negative: Couldn't hold Boasson Hagen's wheel despite being on it exiting roundabout, revealing power deficit against punch-sprinters in finale
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: neutral: Team Sky calculated 23sec cushion sufficient for ITT defense, projecting 45sec-1min advantage over Bardet/Uran in individual effort
